title: qualea cordata var. dichotoma
intro: Qualea cordata var. dichotoma, also known as Quina or Quina bark, is a plant species native to the Amazon rainforest, and is a variant of the Qualea cordata tree. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for centuries, and its medicinal properties include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Qualea cordata var. dichotoma include its potential to treat fever, malaria, and other infectious diseases, as well as its ability to reduce inflammation and improve digestive health. In herbal preparations, the bark of the Qualea cordata var. dichotoma tree is typically dried and used as a tea or powder to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
